title: Flaky Kenyan Peanuts Tagine
description: A rich, savory, and slightly sweet peanut stew originating from Kenya. The combination of peanuts, dashi extract, and spices creates a unique and aromatic flavor profile.
instructions: Heat coconut oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the onion and sauté until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the ginger and garlic and sauté for another minute.
Add the peanuts and cook, stirring frequently, until lightly toasted, about 5 minutes.
Add the cumin, coriander, turmeric, salt, and black pepper, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add 2 cups of water and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened slightly.
Stir in the dashi extract and cook for another minute.
Taste and adjust seasoning as needed.
Garnish with chopped cilantro and serve over rice or with naan bread.
Let the dish rest for 5 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
